answer:
In Charles Dunoyer's *Nouveau traité d'économie* (Vol. I), **climate** (*climat*; no mention of Arabic *iqlim*) functions as a secondary **external circumstance** (*circonstances extérieures*) in analyzing liberty's (*liberté*) uneven distribution across peoples and eras. Liberty arises primarily from internal human forces—racial (*race*) endowments and faculties (*facultés*) perfected through culture (*culture*)—rather than climate, which can only aid or hinder their exercise.[^1] Dunoyer dedicates Chapter 3 ("Influence of external circumstances on liberty") to critiquing overemphasis on climate by Montesquieu, Cabanis, and especially Charles Comte: climate shapes arts (*arts*), customs (*mœurs*), and progress (*progrès*) indirectly (e.g., via soil fertility, temperature moderating needs/laziness, or navigation aiding exchange), but cannot override racial genius (*génie*) or produce moral life (*vie morale*); fertile/hot climes foster laziness (*paresse*) and stagnation (e.g., Pacific islands, India), while harsher ones spur industry (*industrie*) via necessity (*nécessité*).[^2] Peoples in "unfavorable" climes (e.g., Europe) surpass those in favored ones (e.g., Mexico, China) by human agency transforming climate (e.g., cultivation softening cold/wetness).[^3] Chapter 2 notes climes preserve racial traits (e.g., skin color unchanged across latitudes), reinforcing race > climate primacy.[^4] Chapter 6 dismisses Montesquieu's Tartar "liberty" as climate-driven illusion (no forests/marshes for flight), attributing nomad servitude to ignorance/violence.[^5]

In Charles Comte's *Traité de législation* (Vol. II, Ch. 1: "What constitutes the perfection and degradation of man's diverse parts"), **climate** (*climat*) is a key **external influence** on physical/moral/intellectual faculties (*facultés physiques, morales, intellectuelles*), whose perfection/degradation (*perfectionnement/dégradation*) determines prosperity (*prospérité*) /liberty distribution; it modifies organs/sensibility but cannot create racial varieties (*variétés/especes*), with full analysis deferred.[^6] Slavery's necessity in hot climes (Vol. I, Ch. 3) exemplifies false climate determinism, yielding erroneous laws (*lois*) /morals (*mœurs*): equating latitude with despotism paralyzes reform.[^7]

Dunoyer treats Comte's climate emphasis as exaggerated (*exagéré*), subordinating it to race/faculties for liberty's analytic; Comte subordinates it to modifiable causes (contra immutable *iqlim*-like fatalism). Neither work covers *iqlim* explicitly (Arabic/Ptolemaic zone); corpus searches yield only *climat*/*climate*.

[^1]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL I*, Ch. 3, [para p-a30dd3](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/03-ii-influence-des-circonstances-exterieures-sur-la-/translation/#p-a30dd3).

[^2]: *Ibid.*, [Ch. 3, para p-405b54](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/03-ii-influence-des-circonstances-exterieures-sur-la-/translation/#p-405b54); [p-0446c9](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/03-ii-influence-des-circonstances-exterieures-sur-la-/translation/#p-0446c9); [p-a405be](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/03-ii-influence-des-circonstances-exterieures-sur-la-/translation/#p-a405be).

[^3]: *Ibid.*, [Ch. 3, para p-1082cc](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/03-ii-influence-des-circonstances-exterieures-sur-la-/translation/#p-1082cc); [p-08c39c](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/03-ii-influence-des-circonstances-exterieures-sur-la-/translation/#p-08c39c).

[^4]: *Ibid.*, Ch. 2, [para p-d3fec5](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/02-i-influence-de-la-race-sur-la-liberte/translation/#p-d3fec5).

[^5]: *Ibid.*, Ch. 6, [para p-04a90b](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/06-i-liberte-compatible-avec-la-maniere-de-vivre-des-/translation/#p-04a90b).

[^6]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL II*, Ch. 1, [para p-44f18f](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/01-de-ce-qui-constitue-le-perfectionnement-et-la-degr/translation/#p-44f18f).

[^7]: *Ibid.*, Vol. I Ch. 3, [para p-16cb4b](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/03-de-l-influence-qu-exerce-sur-les-moeurs-et-sur-les/translation/#p-16cb4b).
